description: Project scaffolding: create folder structure, requirements.txt, .env.example, and .gitignore. Initialize Python virtual environment.

REVISED - FastAPI server skeleton + dynamic Tripletex client: build POST /solve endpoint that accepts {prompt, proxy_url, session_token, files}. Rewrite TripletexClient to accept per-request credentials (proxy_url + session_token) instead of env vars. Add FastAPI + uvicorn to requirements. Verify server starts and /health returns 200.

Claude tool-calling agent: implement an agentic loop where Claude receives the task prompt and a registry of Tripletex API tools. Claude calls tools in sequence until the task is complete. Tools wrap the dynamic TripletexClient. Support all 7 input languages via Claude's multilingual understanding.

Tripletex tools registry: implement tool definitions for the most common task categories: employees, customers, products, invoices, payments, credit notes, travel expenses, projects, departments, vouchers/ledger. Each tool wraps one or more Tripletex API calls.
File handling: if the /solve request includes PDF or image attachments (base64 or multipart), extract text/images and include them in Claude's context so it can read invoice data from documents.

Error handling and agent guardrails: handle Tripletex API errors (4xx/5xx), rate limits (429), Claude tool errors. Add a hard timeout guard (4.5 min) so we always return before the platform's 5-min cutoff. Log each run to logs/ as JSON.
Efficiency optimization: minimize API round-trips by fetching lookups (VAT types, account codes, currencies) once and caching within a request. Use Claude to batch operations where possible.
Deployment: expose the FastAPI server publicly over HTTPS. Options: Railway, Render, or ngrok for local testing. Document deployment steps in README. Verify the platform can reach /solve.

End-to-end test: simulate a /solve request locally with a known task prompt (e.g. 'Create an employee named X'), verify the correct Tripletex API call is made and returns 200. Cover at least 3 task categories.
